mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in retail sales indicates strengthening household demand and higher expected corporate revenues; higher 3-month growth in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS value available before 2011-01-15 was 380869
failure_condition: Reject if the estimated coefficient on 3-month RSAFS growth is negative, or if positive RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in unemployment from a high level signals improving labor income and recession-risk compression; when UNRATE falls after exceeding 8%, the S&P 500 earns positive forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 8.0 and UNRATE declines by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE value available before 2011-01-15 was 9.4
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations, or if the hit rate for positive 60-day returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, accelerating CPI with a pinned FEDFUNDS rate reduces real policy rates but also raises margin and future tightening risk; high CPI momentum combined with FEDFUNDS below 0.5 predicts negative 20-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5 and CPIAUCSL rises at an annualized rate above 3.0% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS value available before 2011-01-15 was 0.18
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL momentum and low FEDFUNDS has a positive sign, or if triggered cases deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving operating leverage and stronger goods-sector earnings; higher 3-month growth in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations adjust.
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO value available before 2011-01-15 was 94.8756
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month INDPRO growth has a negative walk-forward coefficient for 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if its Rank IC is below 0.03 in every rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently depressed housing starts signal weak residential investment, impaired household balance sheets, and constrained credit creation; HOUST below a low threshold predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST value available before 2011-01-15 was 555
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-600 episodes are followed by higher mean 60-day S&P 500 returns than all other months, or if the negative return hit rate is below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth accompanied by a falling unemployment rate confirms broad labor-market healing rather than participation noise; PAYEMS growth with declining UNRATE predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases over 3 months and UNRATE declines over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS value available before 2011-01-15 was 130712
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform cases where only one labor series improves, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate pinned near zero lowers discount rates and supports risk-taking; FEDFUNDS below 0.25 for several months predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days unless growth deterioration dominates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25 for at least 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS value available before 2011-01-15 was 0.18
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS-below-0.25 periods have lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than higher-rate periods, or if the positive-return hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real-activity confirmation from GDP and retail sales indicates that top-line demand is broad rather than confined to one sector; rising GDPC1 together with rising RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 increases over the latest available quarter and RSAFS increases over the latest 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDPC1 value available before 2011-01-15 was 13278.5
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-up and RSAFS-up signal fails to produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than either signal alone, or if the interaction coefficient is negative.
